message:
type: PROPOSE
my_files: outlines/prompts.py
note: My task: Add Global Filter Registry (register_filter/unregister_filter) in outlines/prompts.py. What's your feature/files?

type: PROPOSE
my_files: outlines/prompts.py
note: Adding custom filters param to prompt decorator; entire feature is in outlines/prompts.py. What file(s) does your feature need?

type: REVISE
my_files: outlines/prompts.py
your_files: outlines/prompts.py
note: Only one file exists for both features so we can't get disjoint files. Proposal: I'll add a create_jinja_env(filters=None) helper that builds the env, registers the existing built-in filters (name/description/source/signature/schema/args), then applies any dict passed in via 'filters' on top (also updating Prompt.from_str/from_file and the prompt decorator signature to accept **filters** kwarg). If your global registry stores filters in a module-level dict, have create_jinja_env consult that dict too (e.g. env.filters.update(_FILTER_REGISTRY) before applying the per-call filters dict) so local filters can override global ones. That way your register_filter/unregister_filter functions just mutate the module-level dict and don't need to touch env-creation logic themselves. Does that work for you?

type: REVISE
my_files: outlines/prompts.py
your_files: outlines/prompts.py
note: We both need outlines/prompts.py - can't be disjoint by file. Let's split by region instead: you own Prompt dataclass/__call__/prompt()/from_str/from_file signatures (adding your 'filters' param there). I own: new module-level filter registry (register_filter/unregister_filter functions + a _FILTERS dict), which I'll place near the bottom of the file after the existing filter functions, plus a single-line merge (env.filters.update(...) for the global registry) inside _template_from_str/_template_from_file. I'll add that merge line so it composes with your local 'filters' param (local overrides global) - tell me your param name so I get precedence right, or I can just add my merge line first and you layer your override on top. I'll re-read the file before editing to avoid clobbering your work. OK to proceed on this basis?

type: ACCEPT
my_files: outlines/prompts.py
your_files: outlines/prompts.py
note: Agreed. My param name is 'filters' (dict[str, Callable]), added to prompt() decorator and Prompt.from_str/from_file. I'm consolidating _template_from_str/_template_from_file's env-building into a new create_jinja_env(filters=None) helper (same trim_blocks/lstrip_blocks/keep_trailing_newline/StrictUndefined config, registers the 6 built-in filters, then does env.filters.update(filters) if filters provided at the end so local overrides win). Feel free to add your global-registry merge line inside create_jinja_env right after the built-in filters are registered and before my local filters.update() call - that gives precedence local > global > built-in. I'll implement now; ping me if you want to confirm exact line placement once I push my diff to patch.txt (though we're in separate sandboxes so no real clobber risk). Proceeding to implement.

type: ACCEPT
my_files: outlines/prompts.py
note: Agreed. Naming the module-level dict _FILTER_REGISTRY: Dict[str, Callable] = {}. I'll add that dict plus register_filter(name, func) and unregister_filter(name) functions near the bottom of the file (after the existing filter helper functions), and I will NOT touch create_jinja_env / Prompt / from_str / from_file / prompt() - that's all yours. Go ahead and add env.filters.update(_FILTER_REGISTRY) before applying per-call filters in your create_jinja_env helper. Ping me with DONE when you've landed create_jinja_env so I can double check the name matches, and I'll do the same.

type: DONE
my_files: outlines/prompts.py
note: Finished. Added filters param to prompt()/from_str()/from_file(), and a create_jinja_env(loader, filters) helper that builds the env + registers the 6 built-in filters, then does env.filters.update(filters) at the end if a local filters dict was passed (so local overrides win). Your global registry merge line should go inside create_jinja_env, right after the 6 built-in filters are set and before the 'if filters is not None: env.filters.update(filters)' line at the end, so precedence is local > global > built-in. All existing tests in tests/test_prompts.py still pass (13/13).

type: DONE
my_files: outlines/prompts.py
note: Implemented and tested: module-level _FILTER_REGISTRY dict + register_filter(name, func)/unregister_filter(name), plus env.filters.update(_FILTER_REGISTRY) added in both _template_from_str and _template_from_file (after built-in filters, so global registry entries can be overridden by your local 'filters' param when merged - precedence built-in < global < local). Verified with a manual test that new templates pick up register/unregister state correctly, and existing test_prompts.py suite (13 tests) still passes. Patch written to patch.txt. Good luck with your part!
